plan: Step 1: Create the site generation orchestrator — `src/domain/generate-site.ts`. This pure function takes a seed string, uses `deriveSeed`/`createRng` to deterministically pick an Era (from `eras`), Archetype (filtered by era compatibility), and Region (filtered by archetype compatibility). It then calls `selectTheme(seed, era)` and `generateMetadata(seed, era, archetype, region)` and returns a `GeneratedSite` object containing `{ seed, era, archetype, region, theme, metadata }`. Export a new `GeneratedSite` interface from `src/domain/types.ts`.

Step 2: Create `src/render/apply-theme.ts` — a function `applyTheme(container: HTMLElement, theme: Theme): void` that sets CSS custom properties on the container element derived from the Theme object (e.g., `--font-family`, `--heading-font`, `--color-primary`, `--color-background`, `--color-text`, `--spacing-unit`, `--border-radius`, `--border-style`, etc.). This converts the Theme JS object into live CSS variables so all rendered sections inherit the theme.

Step 3: Create `src/render/render-header.ts` — a function `renderHeader(site: GeneratedSite): HTMLElement` that builds a `<header>` element containing the site name as a logo/text, and a `<nav>` with 3–5 plausible navigation links derived from the archetype and region (using `region.vocabularyOverrides` for localized labels like 'Startseite', 'Impressum'). Link labels should be era-appropriate (e.g., 'Guestbook' for early-web, 'Get Started' for startup-minimalism). Uses the archetype to pick contextually relevant nav items (e.g., 'Flights' for budget-airline, 'Downloads' for os-vendor).

Step 4: Create `src/render/render-hero.ts` — a function `renderHero(site: GeneratedSite): HTMLElement` that builds a hero `<section>` containing: the tagline (if present) as a heading, the summary text as a paragraph, and optionally a call-to-action button with era-appropriate text (e.g., 'Enter Site' for early-web, 'Sign Up Free' for startup-minimalism). The hero section's structure should feel appropriate to the era — simple for early-web, prominent for startup-minimalism.

Step 5: Create `src/render/render-footer.ts` — a function `renderFooter(site: GeneratedSite): HTMLElement` that builds a `<footer>` element containing: a copyright line with the company name and `yearFounded`, the site's status label (e.g., 'Permanently Offline'), and the closure reason. For German regions, use vocabulary overrides for 'Impressum'/'Datenschutz' links. Include era-appropriate footer elements like 'Best viewed in Netscape' for early-web or 'Powered by' for portal-era.

Step 6: Create `src/render/render-homepage.ts` — the main render orchestrator. Function `renderHomepage(container: HTMLElement, site: GeneratedSite): void` that: (1) clears the container's innerHTML, (2) calls `applyTheme(container, site.theme)`, (3) creates a wrapper div, (4) appends `renderHeader(site)`, `renderHero(site)`, and `renderFooter(site)` into the wrapper, (5) appends the wrapper to the container. Also create a barrel export `src/render/index.ts`.

Step 7: Create `src/styles/generated-site.css` — base styles for the rendered homepage that consume the CSS custom properties set by `applyTheme`. Includes styles for the wrapper, header, nav, hero section, footer, and basic responsive rules. All colors, fonts, spacing, and borders reference CSS variables so they adapt to whatever theme is active.

Step 8: Wire everything into `src/main.ts` — import `generateSite` and `renderHomepage`, and after resolving the seed, call `const site = generateSite(seed)` then `renderHomepage(generatedSite, site)` where `generatedSite` is the existing `#generated-site` container. Import the new `generated-site.css` stylesheet.

Step 9: Write tests in `tests/us-010-render-homepage.test.ts` covering: (a) `generateSite` returns valid GeneratedSite with all required fields for any seed, (b) `generateSite` is deterministic (same seed → same output), (c) `renderHomepage` produces a DOM with `<header>`, hero `<section>`, and `<footer>` elements, (d) header contains site name and nav links, (e) hero contains tagline/summary text, (f) footer contains copyright with year and status label, (g) no errors thrown for multiple different seeds (smoke test across 10+ seeds), (h) theme CSS custom properties are applied to container.
Step 10: Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to verify everything passes with zero errors.
testing_recommendations: Determinism test: call `generateSite` with the same seed 3 times, assert all outputs are deeply equal
DOM structure test: render a homepage in jsdom, query for `header`, `section` (hero), and `footer` — all must exist
Header content test: assert header contains an element with the site name text and a `nav` with at least 3 child links
Hero content test: assert hero section contains the metadata summary text; if tagline exists, assert it appears
Footer content test: assert footer contains the yearFounded and statusLabel from metadata
Theme application test: after render, assert CSS custom properties (e.g., `--color-primary`, `--font-family`) are set on the container via `getComputedStyle` or `style.getPropertyValue`
Multi-seed smoke test: loop over 20 random seeds, call generateSite + renderHomepage for each, assert no exceptions thrown and all 3 sections present
Region vocabulary test: generate a site with a de-de region seed, verify nav uses German labels from vocabularyOverrides
TypeScript compilation: `npm run typecheck` passes with no errors
Lint: `npm run lint` passes with no errors
risks: Nav link and CTA text selection logic needs careful design — labels must be era- and archetype-appropriate without hardcoding too many combinations. Mitigate by defining small per-archetype nav-item arrays in data and using the RNG to pick from them.

CSS custom property approach must work in jsdom for tests — jsdom supports `element.style.setProperty()` but `getComputedStyle` may not fully resolve custom properties. Tests should check `style.getPropertyValue` directly on the container rather than relying on computed styles.

The orchestrator (`generateSite`) introduces a new selection step for era/archetype/region that doesn't yet exist — need to ensure the pick logic correctly filters archetypes by era compatibility and regions by archetype compatibility to avoid impossible combinations.

Generated CSS styles must not conflict with existing shell.css or overlay-ui z-index layering — the rendered content lives inside `#generated-site` which is already z-index: 1 and positioned correctly, so scoping styles to that container avoids conflicts.
